compositionGate.summary: Overall composition matches the Figma design. Desktop layout shows heading on the left and social media links on the right in a horizontal row, separated by a top border. The proportions, visual weight, and element positions all match the design.

crossBrowser.notes: All three browsers render identically at 1440px. No cross-browser rendering discrepancies observed.

layout.desktop: Heading 'Follow Us' on the left, social media links on the right. Flex container with justify-between distributes elements correctly. Matches Figma layout precisely.
layout.tablet: At 768px, layout transitions to horizontal via md:flex-row. Heading and nav share the same row. Social items display horizontally.
layout.mobile: At 375px, items stack vertically. Heading on top, followed by each social link stacked below, left-aligned. Matches spec requirement: 'On mobile display one item below the other, aligned to the left.'

findings.notes: Heading appears visually correct -- bold, slanted Anton font, appropriate size. The 0.05 line-height difference is imperceptible.

findings.difference: None (exact match for size, weight, and line-height)
findings.notes: Label text renders at the correct small size with medium weight. Font family maps from Roboto to the theme's sans-serif (General Sans), which is the standard theme mapping.
Border line appears as a dark charcoal separator, matching the Figma design.

borderRadius.notes: No border-radius in the Figma design. Implementation has none. Correct.

alignment.notes: Desktop: heading and social links are vertically centered within the flex container (md:items-center). Social links are pushed to the right via justify-between. Mobile: all content is left-aligned.

proportions.notes: Icon containers are 24x24px (w-6 h-6) matching the Figma 24x24px icon size. Heading width is flexible, social links use shrink-0 to prevent compression.

default.notes: Minimal content with heading and two social items renders correctly.

fullContent.notes: Four social media links display in a single horizontal row on desktop, matching the Figma design.

manyItems.notes: Eight social items fit on a single row at 1440px. Flex-wrap is available if items need to wrap on narrower viewports.

longHeading.notes: Long heading text wraps naturally. The social links remain on the right via shrink-0 on the nav element.

noHeading.notes: When heading is omitted, social links render correctly aligned to the left without a heading.

singleItem.notes: Single item renders correctly with heading on left and one social link on the right.

notes.description: Social media icons are not visible in the test page because no actual SVG media files are uploaded (logoId is empty). The code correctly handles icons conditionally -- when logoId is set and a media attachment exists, the icon renders via theme_output_svg_or_img. This is a test page content limitation, not a code defect. The Figma shows icons (Facebook, Instagram, TikTok, YouTube logos) before each label text.
notes.impact: Visual only -- icons would appear with actual uploaded SVG media. The icon container (w-6 h-6) and gap (gap-3) are coded correctly per Figma specs.

notes.description: Heading line-height difference: Figma 1.15 vs implementation 1.2 (text-header-5 theme token). The 0.05 difference is imperceptible at 24px font size (approximately 1.2px difference) and is within the 2-4px semantic tolerance.
